ALTER VIEW view1 RENAME TO view2;
ALTER VIEW viewA RENAME TO viewB;
ALTER VIEW viewX RENAME TO viewY;
ALTER VIEW view1 AS SELECT * FROM table1 WHERE id > 10;
ALTER VIEW view2 AS SELECT name, age FROM users WHERE age < 30;
ALTER VIEW view3 AS SELECT product, SUM(sales) FROM sales_data GROUP BY product;
ALTER VIEW view4 AS SELECT o.order_id, c.customer_name FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id;
ALTER VIEW view5 AS SELECT id, avg(score) FROM test_scores GROUP BY id HAVING avg(score) > 75;
CREATE VIEW view1 AS SELECT * FROM table1;
CREATE VIEW view2 AS SELECT id, COUNT(*) FROM table2 GROUP BY id;
ALTER VIEW view1 AS SELECT SUM(value) FROM (SELECT value FROM transactions WHERE type='debit') AS t;
ALTER VIEW view2 AS SELECT o.order_id, p.product_name FROM orders o JOIN products p ON o.product_id = p.product_id WHERE o.status = 'shipped';
ALTER VIEW view1 RENAME TO new_view1;
ALTER VIEW complex_view AS SELECT a.col1, b.col2 FROM tableA a JOIN (SELECT * FROM tableB WHERE condition) b ON a.id = b.foreign_id;
ALTER VIEW final_view AS SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT user_id FROM clicks) AS subquery;
